name: Goscript
description: An alternative implementation of Go language specs written in Rust for embedding as a scripting language

implementation.comment: 64.2% Rust, 35.8% Go (test files); no C/C++ dependencies

language-family.comment: Go syntax - not in the predefined families but closest to imperative/C-family

latest-version.comment: Unstable alpha release; GitHub has newer unreleased code

typing.comment: Go's static type system with type inference

paradigm.comment: Go-style: primarily imperative with some functional aspects (closures)

closures.comment: Go supports closures with variable capture

pattern-matching.comment: Go does not have pattern matching; uses type switches instead

error-handling.comment: Go-style error handling: multiple return values with error as last value

async-await.comment: Uses goroutines for concurrency, not async/await syntax

iterators-generators.comment: Go uses range loops but has no iterator protocol or generators

standard-library.comment: Partial stdlib port described as 'not fully working'; significant work remains

hot-reloading.comment: Not documented or mentioned in repository

source-maps.comment: Unable to verify from available documentation

startup-time.comment: Pure Rust VM with no JIT warmup; lightweight initialization

execution-speed.comment: README states 'you can only expect performance comparable to Python'

api-safety.comment: Pure Rust implementation; unsafe likely contained internally

idiomatic-api.comment: 0% API documentation coverage; basic Config/Engine structs only

async-compatible.comment: Goroutines are internal concurrency; no Rust async runtime integration

function-registration.comment: FFI system requires manual setup; no derive macros documented

type-mapping.comment: No automatic type mapping documented; FFI requires explicit handling

no-std-compatible.comment: Standard library dependencies; no no_std support documented

wasm-compatible.comment: Not documented; cross-platform targets mentioned but WASM unverified

blank-slate-mode.comment: Unable to verify from available documentation

memory-sandboxing.comment: Go semantics prevent arbitrary memory access; designed for embedding

syscall-restrictions.comment: Designed for sandboxed embedding; stdlib partially ported

call-rust-from-script.comment: FFI (Foreign Function Interface) support for calling Rust from scripts

call-script-from-rust.comment: Primary use case is embedding Go scripts in Rust applications

share-data.comment: Data sharing likely requires copying; zero-copy not documented

custom-types.comment: FFI allows exposing custom types to scripts

operator-overloading.comment: Go does not support operator overloading

last-release.comment: Crates.io not updated since initial release; GitHub has newer unreleased code

editor-support.comment: No editor tooling or IDE support documented

example-projects.comment: Positioned like Lua in Redis/WoW or Python in Sublime Text

maintenance-status.comment: Last commit September 2023; no crates.io updates since 2021; 814 commits total

documentation-quality.comment: 0% API documentation coverage; README is only substantial documentation

bus-factor.comment: 5 contributors but development dominated by single author

maturity.comment: Self-described as alpha release; 'considered buggy'; standard library incomplete

primary-use-cases.comment: Designed for embedding Go scripts in Rust applications
